blurb: Marksmanship is the control hunter: Silencing Shot interrupts, Aimed Shot cuts healing, and scatter-trap chains set up kills for your partner. It demands more positioning discipline than BM and rewards it with the most spammable utility kit a physical DPS has in TBC arena.
statPriorityRationale: Marksmanship trades pet safety for Aimed Shot's healing debuff and Silencing Shot — burst-window stats (AP, agility, crit) rank higher than for BM, but resilience still anchors the list.

faq.question: What rating are these Marksmanship Hunters?
faq.answer: This snapshot covers 8 Marksmanship Hunters between 2392 and 2742 rating (average 2592) on the TBC Classic arena ladder, refreshed from public leaderboard data.

faq.question: What is the most-used Marksmanship Hunter PvP gear right now?
faq.answer: The highest-consensus picks are Merciless Gladiator's Chain Spaulders (100%), Veteran's Chain Bracers (100%), Merciless Gladiator's Chain Gauntlets (100%). Anything above ~70% usage at this rating range is effectively mandatory.

faq.question: How much of the Marksmanship Hunter BiS list is PvP gear vs raid gear?
faq.answer: 67% of the most-used slots are arena/honor items — for Marksmanship Hunters the resilience on PvP gear beats raid alternatives almost everywhere. The exceptions are listed as alternatives per slot.

faq.question: What weapon do Marksmanship Hunters use in arena?
faq.answer: Merciless Gladiator's Cleaver is the main-hand of choice (38% of surveyed players).

faq.question: What gems do Marksmanship Hunters socket for PvP?
faq.answer: Across all sockets, the most-used gems in this snapshot are +4 Hit Rating and +4 Agility, +8 Resilience Rating, +12 Critical Strike Rating & 5% Snare and Root Resist. Socket bonuses are usually ignored unless they align with these.
